task_completion.description: Select the three specified papers in the library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The three papers (Shannon's 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication', Huffman's 'A Method for the Construction of Minimum-Redundancy Codes', and Turing's 'On Computable Numbers...') should be simultaneously highlighted/selected in the Zotero item list.

task_completion.description: Open the export dialog for the selected items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Items' dialog box should be visible on the screen, typically triggered by right-clicking the selected items and choosing 'Export Items...'.

task_completion.description: Choose BibTeX as the export format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Export dialog, the format dropdown menu should display 'BibTeX' as the selected option.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Documents folder in the save dialog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file manager/save dialog should show '/home/ga/Documents' or 'Documents' as the current active directory.

task_completion.description: Save the file with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename 'info_theory_foundations.bib' should be entered into the 'Name' or 'File name' field of the save dialog before confirming the save action.

integrity.description: The agent used the Zotero GUI to perform the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Zotero application interface, not just a terminal or file explorer.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI export.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'echo', 'cat', or 'touch' being used to create or write to 'info_theory_foundations.bib'.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type the BibTeX entries into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no text editors (like gedit, nano, or vim) open with manually typed or pasted BibTeX code for the papers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target papers to be exported are: 1) 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' by Claude E. Shannon, 2) 'A Method for the Construction of Minimum-Redundancy Codes' by David A. Huffman, and 3) 'On Computable Numbers' by Alan Turing. The expected output file is '/home/ga/Documents/info_theory_foundations.bib'. The export format must be BibTeX.
